description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a domed top and a cylindrical body, featuring three prominent hose connection outlets—two on the sides and one facing forward. The hydrant is constructed from robust metal, likely cast iron or steel, and displays visible signs of wear, such as chipped paint and surface scuff marks, indicating long-term outdoor exposure. The top of the hydrant is capped with a metallic operating nut, and the base is wide and flanged for secure mounting to the ground. The outlets are fitted with caps, but there are no visible chains attaching the caps to the hydrant. The surface is painted a bright, slightly weathered red, with some areas showing darker patches from grime or rust. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A weathered red metal fire hydrant with three hose outlets, a domed top, and a wide flanged base, showing signs of outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main hose outlet facing forward, flanked by two side outlets and the domed top with an operating nut.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one of the side hose outlets and the cylindrical body, with the front outlet visible in profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the two side outlets symmetrically, with the main outlet not visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other side outlet and the main outlet in profile.
